name: Upward Transportation LLC
service_types: NEMT / senior & wheelchair-dependent transportation (NOT school bus / SPED)
districts_or_customers: Twin Ports region: Superior, Duluth, Iron River, Brule, Lake Nebagamon, Solon Springs, Gordon, Barnes, Douglas/Bayfield/Washburn/Ashland/St Louis/Carlton/Lake counties; serves elderly and wheelchair clients to medical appointments
recent_news: No M&A or news found; website positions company as door-to-door NEMT provider for seniors/disabled with frequent rider program
fit_notes: Not a school-bus contractor. Specialized NEMT/senior wheelchair transport in MN/WI Twin Ports region. Out of scope for Summit School Services tuck-in (wrong service line, micro-scale, no school routes).

name: School Busing Inc
principals: Unknown — single-entity local USVI school bus contractor
fleet_size_est: ~10-25 buses (sole contracted operator on St. Thomas; size implied by district-wide regular + summer service)
employees_est: unknown (>2; employees field in source likely understated)
service_types: school bus (regular & summer pupil transportation for USVI public schools); also referenced for private schools (VIMSIA)
districts_or_customers: USVI Department of Education / VI Board of Education (St. Thomas island-wide); VIMSIA private school
recent_news: Feb 2025: company warned of significant delays on St. Thomas due to drivers calling in sick. Has been on USVI DOE contract PC051DOE15 (2016-2019, since amended).
fit_notes: USVI-only operator. Geographically far outside Summit's footprint; small island market with single-customer USVI DOE concentration risk and operational issues. Very low strategic fit for tuck-in.

name: Okumura Bus Svc Inc
principals: Unknown (presumed Okumura family; no public principals identified)
service_types: likely school bus / charter (Maui Upcountry); not confirmed on current HI DOE contractor lists
districts_or_customers: Hawaii DOE Maui (Upcountry / Makawao area) — unconfirmed; HI DOE Maui contracts largely held by Ground Transport and Roberts Hawaii
recent_news: No recent news or M&A found. Not visible on current HI DOE Maui school transportation contractor lists; may be inactive, very small private charter, or family operation
fit_notes: Micro-operator (2 employees) on Maui with no online footprint. Hawaii market dominated by Ground Transport and Roberts Hawaii. Too small and geographically isolated to be relevant Summit tuck-in target.

name: Cherryco Transportation Buses (Cherryco Buses Inc)
principals: Charles Cherry (Primary Officer, age ~98); Frankie Squires (Officer 2)
service_types: charter / activity bus services (FMCSA classification); school-bus marketing per Yelp / business directories but no district contracts identified
districts_or_customers: No school district customers publicly identified; appears to be charter/activity bus serving Louisville area
recent_news: No recent M&A. Company incorporated KY since 1952; FMCSA registration since Dec 1993; revenue est. <$500K
fit_notes: Single-bus owner-operator (1 truck/1 driver per FMCSA) with founder Charles Cherry at ~98 years old — extreme succession trigger, but business is too small to be a meaningful tuck-in. Could possibly be acquired purely for KY operating authority; otherwise immaterial.

name: D & N Bus Service Inc
principals: Ryan Moore (operator/owner); founded by the late Neil Moore (d. 2015)
fleet_size_est: ~17-20 buses (operates 17 numbered routes for Milford School District — largest single contractor in that district)
districts_or_customers: Milford School District, DE (Sussex County) — routes 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 11, 12, 15, 23, 25, 29, 34, 39, 45, 46, 54
recent_news: Founder Neil Moore passed away Dec 2015; son Ryan Moore now operates the business. Continues as Milford SD's largest bus contractor.
fit_notes: Genuine independent family-owned DE school-bus contractor with 17 routes at Milford SD (single-district concentration). Second-generation operator (Ryan Moore) post-founder death — plausible mid-term succession candidate. Delaware/Sussex County is a credible Mid-Atlantic tuck-in geography for Summit; worth tracking, though small (~$1-3M est. revenue).
